name: Butter Nut Twists
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water (110°F to 115°F), set aside.
2. In a large bowl, mix butter and flour until coarse crumbs form.
3. Add the yeast mixture, beaten eggs, sour milk, sugar, and salt; mix lightly.
4. Divide dough into thirds. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
5. Remove one piece at a time from the refrigerator; roll out on a sugared board to a 12x9-inch rectangle.
6. Combine all filling ingredients in a separate bowl.
7. Sprinkle 1/3 cup of filling on half of the 12-inch edge of dough. Fold over lengthwise and seal, forming a 12x4-1/2-inch rectangle.
8. Pat out the filling into the dough to press it evenly.
9. Sprinkle another 1/3 cup of filling on half of the 12-inch edge of dough. Fold over lengthwise, forming a 12x2-inch rectangle.
10. Press down to form a 12x4-inch rectangle.
11. Slice 1/2-inch pieces along the 12-inch side of the dough.
12. Repeat with remaining two portions of dough.
13. Twist each slice and roll in the remaining filling.
14. Place on greased baking sheets.
15. Bake at 350°F for 15-18 minutes or until golden brown.
16. Serve warm or cool.
(To make sour milk, place 2 tablespoons white vinegar in a measuring cup. Add enough milk to equal 3/4 cup.)
